items.title: Include bias and verify separability
items.avoid: Training w-only models on tasks that need an offset, then blaming the learning rate when accuracy stalls.
items.prefer: Use z = wᵀx + b and plot 2D data with the learned line; if error persists, test XOR-style non-separability.
items.why: Bias shifts the hyperplane; separability is a data geometry question independent of η.
items.example: Fit AND with learnable intercept; if XOR labels fail, switch to MLP instead of increasing max_iter.

items.title: Scale features before training
items.avoid: Feeding raw mixed-scale features into sklearn Perceptron or hand-rolled updates.
items.prefer: StandardScaler in a pipeline, then fit with consistent η.
items.why: Balanced features make the dot product and mistake updates fair across dimensions.

items.title: Pick the right linear model for the job
items.avoid: Using a perceptron when calibrated probabilities or max-margin robustness is required.
items.prefer: Perceptron for teaching and simple separable baselines; logistic regression for probabilities; linear SVM for margin-based separation.
items.why: Step output and mistake-driven rule lack probability calibration and margin optimization.
items.example: Medical triage needing P(disease) → LogisticRegression; wide-margin text baseline → LinearSVC.

items.title: Use pocket or averaged variants on noisy data
items.avoid: Running vanilla perceptron indefinitely on overlapping classes expecting convergence.
items.prefer: Pocket algorithm (keep best-so-far weights), averaged perceptron, or switch to soft-margin SVM / logistic loss.
items.why: Non-separable data causes weight cycling; best weights may occur mid-training.
items.example: Track weights with lowest training error across epochs when labels overlap in 2D scatter.
